content: Brain tumor treatment is multimodal: surgery, radiation, chemotherapy, tumor-treating fields. Maximal safe surgical resection is first-line for accessible tumors—improves survival and reduces mass effect. Glioblastoma: Stupp protocol—radiation (60 Gy in 30 fractions) with concurrent temozolomide (75 mg/m² daily), followed by adjuvant temozolomide (150-200 mg/m² days 1-5 every 28 days × 6-12 cycles). MGMT methylated tumors benefit most from temozolomide. Tumor-treating fields (Optune)—wearable device delivering low-intensity alternating electric fields—improves survival when added to temozolomide (median OS 20.9 vs 16.0 months). Bevacizumab (anti-VEGF) used for recurrent GBM—reduces edema and steroid requirement but no OS benefit. Low-grade gliomas: observation for asymptomatic small lesions; surgery for symptomatic/progressive; radiation and PCV chemotherapy (procarbazine, CCNU, vincristine) or temozolomide for high-risk features. Meningiomas: observation for small asymptomatic; surgery for symptomatic/large; radiation for atypical/malignant or unresectable. Brain metastases: stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) for limited metastases, whole-brain radiation for multiple, surgery for large symptomatic lesions. Supportive care: dexamethasone for edema (taper ASAP), levetiracetam for seizures (no prophylaxis unless seizure occurred), DVT prophylaxis.
